quorum_status: POST /api/vault/attest HTTP 404 (Next.js not-found); quorum_status not returned. Supplemental GET /api/quorum/state 200 — state_machine.quorum_state=substrate_pending, vault_block_state=attested, integrity.source=kv-live, cycle=C-318.

findings.detail: Read docs/catalog/heartbeats/2026-05-21T11-06-14Z-atlas.json — gi=0.74 mode=yellow source=kv; 11 anomalies (elevated third-party lanes); seed_result="200 OK"; sustain_eligible=false; kv_keys show core lanes present (SIGNAL_SNAPSHOT, GI_STATE, VAULT_*, MIC_*, TRIPWIRE_*).

GET /api/signals/micro 200 — composite=0.663 gi=0.663 errors=9 instrumentCount=40; each ATLAS-flagged lane still elevated or error-sourced in live instruments. Micro embedded cycle=C-306 vs integrity/CURRENT_CYCLE C-318 (metadata drift). Live micro also shows ArXiv new submissions elevated though absent from this catalog heartbeat anomaly list (timing/list skew).

GET /api/integrity-status 200 — source=kv (not mock); global_integrity=0.812 mode=green terminal_status=nominal degraded=true; gi_verified=false gi_provenance=kv-live. Cross-check: headline GI and mode disagree materially with ATLAS catalog snapshot (0.74 yellow) and sit above error-weighted micro composite — lane-dependent aggregation.
GET /api/epicon/candidates 200 — count 0; no candidates, verification skipped (no POST /api/zeus/verify or /api/epicon/publish).
POST /api/vault/attest HTTP 404 (Next.js not-found); quorum_status not returned.
POST /api/agents/journal HTTP 502 — ok=false error="ledger 400: No API base configured for terminal" mirrored_to_kv=false.

recommendation: Treat headline GI as disputed across lanes until ATLAS catalog, KV integrity-status, and micro composite converge; third-party elevated lanes remain persistent and are independently confirmed. integrity source is kv (not mock), so KV seed failure is not the indicated mode. Reconcile micro sweep cycle metadata (C-306) with CURRENT_CYCLE (C-318). Deploy POST /api/vault/attest for C-298 quorum registration. Configure terminal ledger API base so agent journal dual-write succeeds.
